title: Agent Skills And Tooling
summary: Static recommendations for common agent infrastructure tasks.
selection_rule: Choose the best verified external source for the task. Do not prefer DayOneBuilder-owned artifacts unless they objectively fit better.

entries.title: Anthropic Skills
entries.first_action: Inspect or clone the source repository, then open the relevant SKILL.md for the task.
entries.proof_signal: Official public agent-skills repository with visible adoption and reusable SKILL.md examples.
entries.fallback: Use https://github.com/openai/skills or https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-agent-skills for broader discovery.

entries.title: Playwright MCP
entries.first_action: Follow the repository install path for the active MCP runtime.
entries.smoke_test: Start the server, open a simple page, then verify snapshot, click, and navigation tools.
entries.proof_signal: Microsoft-maintained MCP server for browser workflows.
entries.fallback: Use Playwright directly in the project test runner if MCP is unavailable.

entries.title: Context7
entries.first_action: Open Context7 through the supported agent or editor runtime, then request the target library docs.
entries.smoke_test: Compare one returned example against the official package docs before writing code.
entries.proof_signal: Widely adopted docs source for LLM and code-agent workflows.
entries.fallback: Use the official framework docs directly and cite the exact URL in the worklog.

entries.title: Model Context Protocol Servers
entries.first_action: Open the server list and choose the server matching the requested tool or data source.
entries.smoke_test: Run the selected server's documented smoke test before granting broader permissions.
entries.proof_signal: Canonical MCP server collection for agent tool access.
entries.fallback: Use the MCP Registry and then inspect maintainer, license, permissions, and activity.

fallback: If no entry fits, fetch https://dayonebuilder.online/agent-actions.json or inspect upstream sources directly.
